comment: Sidekick R3 lane L: scripts/host/host-pressure-proof.sh written (controlled executable pressure + recovery proof). Verifier Gate 9 invokes it; dry-run path is the default (safe), live path requires HPP_LIVE=1 (operator-gated). Lanes I (admission refusal) + J (staged shed) + K (crash harness) are integrated as the recovery mechanisms under pressure.

author: sidekick-reliability-boundary-0725 (lane W)
text: Sidekick R4 lane W: docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh Gate 8 (4) now multiplies each bounded-ceiling source by SOFT_THROTTLE_OVERFLOW_MULT=2 to model transient soft-throttle overshoot. QEMU 34G ceiling -> ~68 GiB worst-case transient peak (kernel throttles around 36 GiB in practice; 2x gives margin). The host reserve equation is now: physical_host_RAM >= (qemu_ceiling * 2) + (ao_mcp_ceiling * 2) + mandatory_reserve. Future bounded top-level slices should follow the same multiplier (comment in 4d explicitly says so). The 'max' fail-closed behavior is preserved. bash -n clean. No commit/push per lane brief.

author: sidekick-reliability-boundary-0725 (lane W)
text: Sidekick R4 lane W (delta from R3 lane H): docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh Gate 8 (4) now applies SOFT_THROTTLE_OVERFLOW_MULT=2 per bounded-ceiling source. The protected-reserve equation (physical_host_RAM >= qemu_worst + ao_mcp_worst + mandatory_reserve) accounts for the fact that MemoryHigh is a soft throttle - the cgroup can transiently overshoot by up to ~1x before the kernel throttles, and 2x gives a safety margin. On this host (QEMU MemoryHigh=34 GiB), worst-case transient QEMU footprint is ~68 GiB; combined with ao_mcp_worst + mandatory_reserve (~6.4 GiB from 10% of 63.6 GiB physical RAM), aggregate worst case exceeds physical RAM - fail-closed until the host RAM grows or QEMU MemoryHigh is lowered. The 'max' (unbounded) cgroup probe is preserved; bash -n clean. No commit/push per lane brief.

comment: Sidekick R4 lane S: scripts/host/host-pressure-proof.sh now defaults to LIVE mode (--live / HPP_LIVE=1). --dry-run exits 64 (non-pass) so the verifier Gate 9 fails without explicit HPP_LIVE. Tightened: any canary failure = FAIL; runner-count loss > 10% = FAIL; require count concurrent canaries (auto-derives from runner_count when --concurrency=0); require admission-refusal alert during peak (opt-out via --no-admission-required). Live mode is operator-gated: default no-flag/no-env invocation exits 33 (HPP_LIVE=1 required); --live flag sets the env so it never bypasses the gate. Verifier Gate 9 catches exit 64 as 'proof not attempted' and FAILs with [FAIL] Gate 9 proof not attempted (HPP_LIVE=1 not set). HPP_SKIP_PRESSURE removed; only --dry-run and HPP_LIVE=1 / --live remain as valid invocation paths. Added trap EXIT for cleanup of stress-ng log, canary temp dir, and dmesg snapshot. Bead: r3f8 (dry-run-as-PASS removed); r3f9 (claims enforced); bjpk (Gate 9 — executable proof defaulting to LIVE).

Sidekick R4 lane S: host-pressure-proof.sh tightened per r3f9 — central claims are now FAIL-conditions rather than warnings: (1) any canary failure (non-zero exit or per-canary timeout) = FAIL, captured via per-canary .rc files in a temp dir; (2) runner-count loss > HPP_RUNNER_LOSS_PCT% (default 10%) = FAIL; (3) zero 'runner_pool.memory_pressure' alerts during pressure phase = FAIL (HPP_REQUIRE_ADMISSION_REFUSAL=1, opt-out via --no-admission-required); (4) concurrency auto-derives from CONFIG_RUNNER_COUNT when --concurrency=0 (no more hardcoded N=3). Added trap INT/TERM/EXIT for cleanup. Verifier Gate 9 catches these exits 1 as Gate 9 FAIL.

Sidekick R4 lane S: bead bjpk (Gate 9 — executable physical-host availability proof). Verifier Gate 9 rewritten: --dry-run exits 64 (refusal, not a pass) → Gate 9 FAILs by default with [FAIL] Gate 9 proof not attempted (HPP_LIVE=1 not set); HPP_LIVE=1 + exit 0 = PASS; HPP_LIVE=1 + exit 1 = FAIL (r3f9 enforcer: canary / runner-loss / admission-refusal / OOM / recovery-fail); HPP_LIVE=1 + exit 2 = precondition FAIL with remediation pointer. Mac parity preserved via --mac-hostname forwarding on both paths. HPP_SKIP_PRESSURE legacy env var removed; only --dry-run and HPP_LIVE=1 (or new --live alias) are valid invocation paths.

Sidekick R4 lane R: PASS. AO drop-in installed at /home/jleechan/.config/systemd/user/ao-daemon.service.d/memory.conf (65 bytes) with [Service] MemoryHigh=20G MemoryAccounting=yes Slice=agents.slice. systemctl --user daemon-reload exit=0. systemctl --user restart ao-daemon.service exit=0. Post-restart: MainPID=472555 cgroup=0::/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/agents.slice/ao-daemon.service (correctly ends with /agents.slice/). systemctl --user show ao-daemon.service confirms Slice=agents.slice MemoryAccounting=yes MemoryHigh=21474836480 (=20GiB). agents.slice memory.high=21474836480 (20 GiB ceiling live). agent-auto-migrate.sh status --verbose reports 1 leaf (ao-daemon.service high=21474836480 current=70MB procs=9). agent-auto-migrate.sh apply --dry-run identified 9 candidates (claude/codex/codex-code-mode-host) outside the slice (live migration is out of scope for lane R — SIGTERMs user sessions; dry-run proves the helper is wired correctly). r3f4 (drop-in + enrollment) is the lane R install scope; r3f7 (verifier logic inversion) is also addressed by the helper's per-process [target]/[skip] output — SET-based, not leaf-count. Lane R does NOT close r3f7 because Gate 8 verifier-logic rework is a code change owned by the parent sidekick.

Sidekick R4 lane R: install-side delta addressing r3f7 (verifier logic inversion). The agent-auto-migrate helper now uses SET-based detection (pgrep by basename match against claude/codex/gemini/cursor/aider/cody) instead of leaf counting — apply --dry-run enumerates each PID individually as [target] (outside agents.slice) or [skip] (already in agents.slice). Live observation on this host: 14 candidate processes total = 8 [target] outside slice + 6 [skip] inside slice, of which 5 are children of the freshly-restarted ao-daemon.service and now live at /agents.slice/ao-daemon.service/. This SET-based per-PID result is exactly the input r3f7 asks the verifier to consume. Lane R only installs the helper's enrollment mechanism; closing r3f7 (rewriting Gate 8 to consume this SET-based output instead of the leaf-count path) is a verifier code change owned by the parent sidekick.

Sidekick R4 lane R: round-4 delta for ez-gh-actions-0725 (host PSI/slice). Lane R installed the AO containment leg: (a) /home/jleechan/.config/systemd/user/ao-daemon.service.d/memory.conf committed by round-3 lane G is now copied to user-scope systemd and active; (b) ao-daemon.service restarted so the slice assignment took effect (PID 472555 in /agents.slice/ao-daemon.service/); (c) live cgroup inheritance verified via systemctl --user show: Slice=agents.slice MemoryHigh=21474836480 MemoryAccounting=yes; (d) agents.slice ceiling is 21474836480 (= 20 GiB) confirmed at /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/agents.slice/memory.high. NOTE for parent sidekick: the task spec expected agents.slice under /app.slice/agents.slice but the actual cgroup v2 hierarchy is /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/agents.slice/ — the spec's path was wrong; the AO drop-in landed correctly regardless. PSI/earlyoom/oomd and NVMe swapfile legs are out of lane R scope (Lane T owns psi-oom-watcher; swapfile work is separate).

author: sidekick-reliability-boundary-0725/lane-T
text: Sidekick R4 lane T: psi-oom-watcher.sh now defaults (constant DEFAULT_PSI_SHED_CHAIN) to drain,reclaim,verify,escalate; empty/unset value fails-closed (exit 64) at timer invocation AND in --shed CLI mode (validator _validate_shed_chain called both at the polling entry and at shed_mode branch). --shed --dry-run and incomplete chains warn-but-still-print-plan so the verifier can SEE the dry-run output. systemd/psi-oom-watcher.service updated with Environment="PSI_SHED_CHAIN=drain,reclaim,verify,escalate" (and Environment="SHED_RECLAIM_TARGET_BYTES=8589934592"). docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh Gate 7 asserts psi-oom-watcher.service Environment has non-empty PSI_SHED_CHAIN when the unit is installed (fail if empty; tolerate unit-absent since install is manual per bead).

author: sidekick-reliability-boundary-0725/lane-T
text: Sidekick R4 lane T: psi-oom-watcher.sh evidence capture fixed — baseline RSS now captured in run_shed_stages BEFORE stage1_drain (SHED_BASELINE_RSS), not after, so stage3_verify measures an honest delta instead of a near-zero post-drain self-comparison. memory.reclaim now writes SHED_RECLAIM_TARGET_BYTES (default 8589934592 = 8 GiB) instead of the prior single-byte '1', which the kernel interpreted as a no-op; documented why a multi-GiB target is required and that '0' / MemoryHigh-clamp are alternative forms. Walkthrough on jeff-ubuntu confirmed pre-drain=21991940 kB post-reclaim=17200280 kB drop=4791660 kB (~4.6 GiB, exceeds 1 GiB threshold, SHED_RESULT=ok).

author: sidekick-reliability-boundary-0725/lane-T
text: Sidekick R4 lane T follow-up: child beads r3f10 + r3f11 fixed. Stage-chain (drain/reclaim/verify/escalate) is now fail-closed (exit 64) on empty / unset / incomplete PSI_SHED_CHAIN at polling entry and --shed mode; service unit Environment= line carried by docs/host-ops-sudo-block-0725.md's sed-copy install step. memory.reclaim target upgraded to multi-GiB (default 8 GiB, configurable via SHED_RECLAIM_TARGET_BYTES) so the kernel actually reclaims rather than treating '1' as a no-op. Pre-drain baseline RSS is now captured up-front, restoring honest verify delta. Defer deploy to single-writer sidekick.
